registered dietitians are experts in food and nutrition. they help people choose foods to eat for their particular needs and bodies. they may also plan menus, offer advice and assessments, educate people on healthy lifestyle choices and more. they often work closely with individuals who have medical or dietary needs. a nutritionist and a registered dietitian are similar. but legally, to call yourself a registered dietitian, you have to fulfill more specific requirements. these include 1200 hours of supervised internship and passing a national exam. which of these best describes the difference between a nutritionist and a registered dietitian? choose one option a a registered dietitian completes fewer hours of a supervised internship than a nutritionist does. b there are legal requirements to become a registered dietitian. c nutritionists do not educate people on healthy eating. d registered dietitians do not need to pass any exams.
